title: David Fifita (rugby league, born 2000)
wikitext:
{{short description|Australian rugby league footballer}} {{Use dmy dates|date=August 2019}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = David Fifita |fullname = David Fifita Jnr |image = David Fifita Tonga.jpg |image_size = |position = {{rlp|SR}} | birth_date = {{Birth date and age|2000|02|25|df=y}} | birth_place = [[Brisbane, Queensland]], Australia | height = {{convert|186|cm|ftin|abbr=on}} | weight = {{convert|115|kg|stlb|abbr=on}} | club1 = [[Brisbane Broncos]] | year1start = 2018 | year1end = 20 | appearances1 = 44 | tries1 = 13 | goals1 = 0 | fieldgoals1 = 0 | points1 = 52 |club2 = [[Gold Coast Titans]] |year2start = 2021 |year2end = 25 |appearances2 = 90 |tries2 = 37 |goals2 = 0 |fieldgoals2 = 0 |points2 = 148 |club3 = [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] |year3start = 2026 |year3end = present |appearances3 = 7 |tries3 = 2 |goals3 = 0 |fieldgoals3 = 0 |points3 = 8 |teamA = {{nowrap|[[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]]}} |yearAstart = 2019 |yearAend = 22 |appearancesA = 4 |triesA = 1 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 4 |teamB = [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] |yearBstart = 2019 |yearBend = 23 |appearancesB = 8 |triesB = 1 |goalsB = 0 |fieldgoalsB = 0 |pointsB = 4 |teamC = [[Prime Minister's XIII]] |yearCstart = 2019 |yearCend = |appearancesC = 1 |triesC = 1 |goalsC = 0 |fieldgoalsC = 0 |pointsC = 4 |teamD = [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia 9s]] |yearDstart = 2019 |yearDend = |appearancesD = 4 |triesD = 2 |goalsD = 0 |fieldgoalsD = 0 |pointsD = 8 |teamE = [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] |yearEstart = 2022 |yearEend = |appearancesE = 4 |triesE = 1 |goalsE = 0 |fieldgoalsE = 0 |pointsE = 4 |new = yes |source = [https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/david-fifita--b-/summary.html Rugby League Project] |updated = 11 June 2026 | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | education = [[Keebra Park State High School]] | relatives = [[Tesi Niu]] (cousin)
[[Latu Fifita]] (cousin)
[[Andrew Fifita]] (cousin)
[[David Fifita (rugby league, born 1989)|David Fifita]] (cousin)
[[Solomon Haumono]] (uncle) }} }} '''David Tuiono Fifita''' (born 25 February 2000) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}}er for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL). He previously played for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] and the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the NRL and has played for the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]], [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]], the [[Prime Minister's XIII]] and the [[Tonga national rugby league team]]. ==Early life== Fifita was born in [[Brisbane]], [[Queensland]], Australia, and is of [[Torres Strait Islander]] ([[Badu people|Badu]]) descent on his mother's side and [[Tongans|Tongan]] descent on his father's side.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/05/brisbane-broncos-rookie-david-fifita-takes-4.57am-train-to-the-top/|title = Fifita takes 4.57am train to the top|date = 4 September 2018}} Fifita was educated at [[Keebra Park State High School]] and his playing style had been compared to former Bronco [[Ben Te'o]]. NRL commentator [[Andrew Voss]] rated Fifita as the best young schoolboy forward he has seen in over 20 years of calling the [[GIO Schoolboy Cup]].{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/02/13/brisbane-broncos-re-sign-david-fifita-until-end-of-2020-nrl-season/|title=Brisbane Broncos re-sign David Fifita until end of 2020 NRL season|date=12 February 2018|website=National Rugby League}} == Playing career== ===Early career=== Fifita played his junior rugby league for Souths Acacia Ridge. In 2015, he was 18th man for the Queensland Under-16 rugby league team, despite being a year younger. He then competed at the ASSRL U15s Tournament, playing for Queensland U15s Schoolboys Maroons team. He was selected for the Australian U15s Schoolboys Merit team. In 2016, he was once again selected for the Queensland Under-16 rugby league team at {{rlp|lk}} and captained the side. He was part of the Keebra Park side, which also featured [[Payne Haas]], that narrowly lost to [[Westfields Sports High School]] in the GIO Schoolboy Cup Final.{{cite web|url=https://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/keebra-park-suffers-heartbreaking-lastminute-loss-to-westfields-sports-high-in-gio-cup-final/news-story/88549c5249a9b1f3613d1ef0f1554a20|title=Keebra finds silver lining in Cup loss|date=14 September 2016|website=www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au}} In 2017, Fifita competed at the ASSRL U18s Tournament, playing for Queensland U18s Schoolboys team. He was chosen for the Australian U18s Schoolboys team, which toured New Zealand. Later in the year, he captained Keebra Park in the GIO Schoolboy Cup Final against Westfields Sports High School, which Keebra Park won.{{cite web|url=https://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/local-sport/keebra-park-defeat-westfields-sports-high-2512-to-win-gio-schoolboys-cup-national-final/news-story/3a4f9e03987e6ab48ceb863a76f09bb3|title=Keebra crowned national champions|date=6 September 2017|website=www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au}} ===2018=== On 13 February 2018, extended his contract with the [[Brisbane Broncos]] to the end of the [[2020 NRL season]].{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/02/13/brisbane-broncos-re-sign-david-fifita-until-end-of-2020-nrl-season/|title=Brisbane Broncos re-sign David Fifita until end of 2020 NRL season|date=12 February 2018|website=National Rugby League}} Fifita started by the year by playing in the [[Queensland Cup]] for the [[Souths Logan Magpies]].{{cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/20182/01/25/hard-work-paying-off-for-fifita/|title=Hard work paying off for Fifita|date=25 January 2018|website=Queensland Rugby League}} In Round 16 of the [[2018 NRL season]], Fifita made his NRL debut for the Brisbane Broncos against the [[Canberra Raiders]], becoming the first player born in the 2000s to make his NRL debut. Fifita played 47 minutes off the interchange bench during the 26-22 comeback win at [[Suncorp Stadium]] and coach [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]] commented on his impressive debut, "I liked it because he played mistake free and that is all you can ask", "He was good and never let anybody down, He did a really good job for us."{{cite web|url=https://au.sports.yahoo.com/broncos-teenager-set-historic-nrl-debut-055209404.html|title=Broncos teenager makes historic NRL debut|website=au.sports.yahoo.com|date=30 June 2018 }}{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/06/30/bennett-knows-brisbane-rode-their-luck-after-thrilling-win/|title=Bennett knows Brisbane must improve after thrilling win|date=30 June 2018|website=National Rugby League}} In Round 23 against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], Fifita scored his first NRL career try in Brisbane's 38–18 win at Suncorp Stadium.{{cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/broncos/brisbane-broncos-beat-south-sydney-rabbitohs-3818-at-suncorp-stadium/news-story/f6c16b015ee83ed534aea35a0562fe93|title=Bennett's spray as Broncos roll Rabbitohs|date=16 August 2018|website=www.couriermail.com.au}} Fifita finished his promising NRL debut year with him playing in 11 matches and scoring 2 tries for Brisbane in the 2018 NRL season. On 17 December 2018, Fifita was selected in the 15-man emerging Queensland squad.{{cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/20182/12/17/emerging-origin-squad-for-2019-announced/|title=Emerging Origin squad for 2019 announced|date=17 December 2018|website=Queensland Rugby League}} ===2019=== On 15 February 2019, Fifita represented the Indigenous All Stars against the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori All Stars team]], playing off the interchange bench and scoring a try in the 34–14 win at [[AAMI Park]].{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/live-blog-pride-on-the-line-as-all-stars-go-into-battle-for-their-cultures/news-story/61d8d7ea5371e49dcb2577f42dc6d70e|title=Indigenous vs Maori NRL All Stars 2019: Tyrone Roberts, David Fifita|date=15 February 2019}} After having some solid performances leading up to the [[2019 State of Origin series]], Fifita earned selection in the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland Maroons]] squad.{{cite web|url=https://www.theaustralian.com.au/subscribe/news/1/?sourceCode=TAWEB_WRE170_a&dest=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.theaustralian.com.au%2Fsport%2Fnrl%2Fstate-of-origin-david-fifita-among-three-new-faces-in-queensland-squad%2Fnews-story%2F30e96f985618a591648789949f969d75&memtype=anonymous&mode=premium&nk=2a13499ba0d71fae35cca452919c4df4-1560110279|title=Subscribe to The Australian - Newspaper home delivery, website, iPad, iPhone & Android apps|website=www.theaustralian.com.au}} Fifita made history as he was the first player born in the 2000s to play Origin, Queensland Coach [[Kevin Walters]] commenting about his selection, "I rang him and told him he had made the Queensland under 18 team," Walters said on Monday. "He said, 'No, I am too old for that, "I said, 'Well you better come and play Origin then.'"{{cite web|url=https://www.thecourier.com.au/story/6184129/qld-origin-call-to-remember-for-fifita/|title=Qld Origin call to remember for Fifita|first=Laine|last=Clark|date=27 May 2019|website=The Courier}} On 30 September, Fifita was named on the bench for the Australia PM XIII side. On 7 October 2019, Fifita was named at second row for the U23 Junior Australian side. ===2020=== On 27 April, it was revealed that Fifita would be ruled out from playing indefinitely after undergoing knee surgery.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/broncos/nrl-2020-david-fifita-ruled-out-indefinitely-due-to-knee-injury-brisbane-broncos-casualty-ward/news-story/db7862524c6d0ca647545d55572faf04/|title=Broncos young gun David Fifita ruled out indefinitely following knee surgery|date=27 April 2020|website=www.foxsports.com.au}} On July 25, it was announced that Fifita had signed a record three-year deal, worth over $3 million with the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]]. Fifita played nine games for Brisbane in the [[2020 NRL season]] as the club finished last on the table for the first time ever in their history. Brisbane only managed to win only three games for the entire year out of a possible 20 matches.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/09/24/brisbane-get-wooden-spoon-as-cowboys-finish-on-a-high/|title=Brisbane get wooden spoon as North Queensland finish on a high|website=www.nrl.com|date=24 September 2020}} ===2021=== In round 5 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he scored a [[hat-trick]] in the Gold Coast's 42-16 victory over Newcastle.{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/nrl-2021-scores-fixtures-results-ladder-gold-coast-titans-beat-newcastle-knights-david-fifita-hat-trick/f3004917-1261-4e6a-8b82-45d24e030864/|title='Only the beginning': Rampant David Fifita sends ominous warning to NRL rivals after hat-trick|website=wwos.nine.com.au|date=10 April 2021 }} In round 7 against South Sydney, he scored his second [[hat-trick]] of the year in a 30-40 loss.{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-04-23/nrl-parramatta-brisbane-broncos-south-sydney-gold-coast/100087882/|title=Parramatta thrashes Brisbane Broncos 46-6, Benji Marshall inspires South Sydney to 40-30 NRL win over Gold Coast|website=www.abc.net.au|date=23 April 2021}} On 11 May, Fifita was suspended for two matches after being found guilty of a careless high tackle during the club's round 9 victory over [[Wests Tigers]].{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/05/11/titans-blow-as-fifita-cops-two-match-ban-at-judiciary/|title=Titans blow as Fifita cops two-match ban at judiciary|website=www.nrl.com|date=11 May 2021}} Fifita played for Queensland in the first two games of the [[2021 State of Origin series]] before being suspended for game 3.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/05/30/nsw-blues-origin-i-team/|title=NSW Origin team: Luai gets nod for Freddie's fresh-faced Blues|website=www.nrl.com|date=30 May 2021}}{{Cite web|title=NSW Blues win State of Origin series opener 50-6 against Queensland Maroons|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-06-09/state-of-origin-i-live-blog-queensland-maroons-vs-nsw-blues/100199016|access-date=2021-06-09|website=ABC|date=9 June 2021|language=en}} ===2022=== Following the Gold Coast's round 8 loss to [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]], it was revealed that Fifita had suffered a grade 2 [[Medial knee injuries|MCL sprain]] and would miss four matches. He played a total of 18 games as the Gold Coast finished 13th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-casualty-ward-injuries-ins-and-outs-return-dates-adam-reynolds-hia-nose-brisbane-broncos-vs-sharks/news-story/50809857fb93b3876a571a49e44cf35c/|title=Fifita sidelined in Origin blow, full extent of Haas injury revealed: Casualty Ward|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=May 2022 }} === 2023 === Fifita played in all three games of the [[2023 State of Origin series]] for Queensland as they won the shield 2-1. On 11 August, Fifita re-signed with the Gold Coast side until the end of the 2026 NRL season.{{cite web | url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2023/08/11/titans-duo-here-to-stay-on-the-gold-coast/ | title=Titans duo here to stay on the Gold Coast | date=11 August 2023 }} Fifita played a total of 22 matches for the Gold Coast in the [[2023 NRL season]] and scored eight tries as the club finished 14th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-gold-coast-titans-season-review/nel398u2vn7faognqvwnq7g4#:~:text=Did%20the%20Titans%20over%2Ddeliver,out%20of%20the%20finals%20picture./|title=NRL 2023: Gold Coast Titans season review|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=5 September 2023 }} ===2024=== On 9 May, it was announced that Fifita had not taken up his option for a contract extension at the Gold Coast and would leave the club following the conclusion of the [[2024 NRL season]]. On the same day, it was announced he would join the [[Sydney Roosters]] on a multi-year deal.{{cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/david-fifita-drops-bombshell-contract-announcement-on-titans/news-story/48ae09d86b62ec3bfcdf755608a57d53/|title=David Fifita drops bombshell contract announcement on Titans|website=www.news.com.au}} On 15 May, less than a week after announcing that he was joining the Sydney Roosters, Fifita announced that he had enacted the clause in his contract and decided to stay with the Gold Coast Titans until the end of 2026. In the wake of Fifita backflipping on his deal with the Sydney Roosters, [[Nick Politis]] spoke to the media stating “It is hard to understand,” Politis told the [[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|Daily Telegraph]]. "He approached us. And did the same with Penrith. After coming Sydney to meet with Roosters coach [[Trent Robinson]] and the rest of the coaching staff, it's hard to understand how he can display and create uncertainty. We’ve decided withdraw our offer".{{Cite web |date=2024-05-15 |title=Dave stays: How Fifita's change of heart led to Titanic turnaround |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2024/05/15/dave-stays-why-fifita-chose-to-sign-on-with-the-titans/ |access-date=2024-05-15 |website=National Rugby League |language=en}}{{Cite web |date=2024-05-15 |title='He approached us!' Roosters fume over Fifita as details of late night $3m backflip emerge |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-david-fifita-backflips-on-sydney-roosters-resigns-with-gold-coast-titans-contract-future/news-story/a0f3601f790d1d3ed6165d4ee17d0151 |access-date=2024-05-15 |website=Fox Sports |language=en}}{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/news-2024-david-fifita-contract-backflip-sydney-roosters-salary-cap-signings-future-burning-questions/ba9b0a0a-e2db-49db-b004-551b79e9e1a9/|title=David Fifita 'circus' leaves collateral damage at Roosters as scorned star eyes vacated $3.3m contract|website=wwos.nine.com.au|date=15 May 2024 }} Fifita made 20 appearances for the Gold Coast throughout the 2024 NRL season as the club finished 14th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-season-review-gold-coast-titans-analysis-exclusive-20240908-p5k8xf.html/|title=The Mole's end-of-season review: Titans 'perennial enigma' who went missing as Des Hasler 'hallmark' falls flat|website=www.nine.com.au}} ===2025=== Ahead of the Gold Coast's round 9 match against Canterbury, Fifita was left out of the starting 17 by head coach [[Des Hasler]] after Fifita reportedly had a disagreement with Hasler about switching to the left edge of attack. On 7 May, Fifita was ruled out indefinitely with an ankle injury. Fifita returned to the Gold Coast side for their round 19 match against Brisbane. In August, it was announced that Fifita would miss the rest of the [[2025 NRL season]] with injury.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-teams-round-10-team-lists-selections-injuries-suspensions-changes/news-story/596c07d7337b5418abe5d9f5703173fb/|title=Axed $1m star gone indefinitely; Broncos duo have 80 minutes to save jobs — Teams Talking Pts|website=www.foxsports.com.au}} On 29 October, the South Sydney Rabbitohs announced the signing of Fifita on a two-year deal.{{Cite web |date=2025-10-29 |title=Rabbitohs sign David Fifita for Seasons 2026 and 2027 – South Sydney Rabbitohs |url=https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/rabbitohs-sign-david-fifita-for-seasons-2026-and-2027 |access-date=2025-10-29 |website=rabbitohs.com.au |language=en}} ===2026=== In round 1 of the [[2026 NRL season]], Fifita made his club debut for South Sydney in their 40-30 victory over the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]].{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2026-dolphins-v-south-sydney-rabbitohs-teams-late-mail-start-time-where-to-watch-ladder-latrell-mitchell-david-fifita-news/news-story/3681d761fcec154f310d69f1b4f1c917/|title=The Bunnies are back! Latrell masterclass as Souths down Dolphins in 70-point stunner|website=www.foxsports.com.au}} == Statistics == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Year !Team !Games !Tries !Pts |- |2018 | rowspan="3" |{{Leagueicon|Brisbane|16}} [[Brisbane Broncos]] |11 |2 |8 |- |2019 |24 |7 |28 |- |2020 |9 |4 |16 |- |2021 | rowspan="5" |{{Leagueicon|Gold Coast Titans|16}} [[Gold Coast Titans]] |22 |17 |68 |- |2022 |18 |7 |28 |- |2023 |22 |8 |32 |- |2024 |20 |4 |16 |- |2025 |8 |1 |4 |- |2026 ||{{Leagueicon|South Sydney Rabbitohs|16}} [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] |6 |1 |4 |- | |Totals |140 |51 |204 |} ==Controversies== On 9 November 2019, Fifita was arrested in [[Bali]] after an alleged altercation with a bouncer at a nightclub called La Favela. On 11 November, Fifita was released from jail after the bouncer who was allegedly assaulted accepted Fifita's apology. After Fifita left the prison, he spoke to the media saying "I want to thank everyone involved and obviously, the police. I will make a further statement later in the week".{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/11/11/David-Fifita-released-from-Bali-jail/|title=David Fifita released from Bali jail|website=NRL|date=12 November 2019}} Brisbane Broncos CEO Paul White spoke to the media saying "I don't know too many 19-year-old men ... of his background, who have had an experience like that, I know, having a policing background, that being in a cell is not a great experience, and being in one in a foreign country was pretty confronting. He'll learn a big lesson".{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/broncos/stuck-in-isolation-and-forced-to-urinate-in-a-water-bottle-inside-david-fifitas-bali-prison-cell/news-story/5e479e45a9472299341cc5c8f76aa578/|title=Thrown in isolation and forced to urinate in a water bottle: Inside David Fifita's Bali cell|website=Fox Sports|date=12 November 2019}} On 27 December 2020, Fifita was arrested on the Gold Coast, but later released without charge after trespassing following a night of drinking. It was reported that Fifita was invited back to the room of an unidentified female but went to the wrong room at the address, where other people called the police.{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/david-fifita-arrest-gold-coast-titans-trespass-police-nrl-2020/shtmkfgb19d1318l659czprx|title=David Fifita arrested over trespassing incident on Gold Coast|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=27 December 2020 }} In February 2021, the NRL reopened its investigation into the trespassing situation as it was revealed that the Titans had, in breach of salary cap rules, made a payment to the family where Fifita had trespassed. In June 2021, Fifita was fined $20,000 by the league for breaching their alcohol code of conduct and bringing the game into disrepute. This again was related to the December 2020 trespassing incident.{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-06-16/nrl-gold-coast-david-fifita-fined-for-alcohol-related-incident/100220798/|title=NRL fines Gold Coast forward David Fifita $20,000 following alcohol-related incident|website=www.abc.net.au|date=16 June 2021}} On 31 December 2022, it was reported that Fifita had been involved in a verbal altercation with [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]] player [[Reece Walsh]] at a Gold Coast nightclub. It was alleged that the pair had to be separated by security staff.{{cite web|url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/rugby-league-bronco-reece-walsh-and-titan-david-fifita-in-heated-nightclub-confrontation/PGWCCGF3EVCKFLMB6WV6OUIT4E/|title=Rugby League: Bronco Reece Walsh and Titan David Fifita in heated nightclub confrontation|website=www.nzherald.co.nz}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/teams/teams-details?id=1733&league=111 South Sydney Rabbitohs profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20250907183844/https://www.titans.com.au/teams/nrl-premiership/gold-coast-titans/david-fifita/ Gold Coast Titans profile] (archived) *[https://web.archive.org/web/20201101021256/https://www.broncos.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/brisbane-broncos/david-fifita/ Brisbane Broncos profile] (archived) {{South Sydney Rabbitohs squad}} {{Australia 2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s squad}} {{Tonga 2021 Rugby League World Cup squad}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Fifita, David}} [[Category:2000 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:21st-century Australian sportsmen]] [[Category:21st-century Indigenous Australian people]] [[Category:Australian rugby league players]] [[Category:Australian people of Tongan descent]] [[Category:Sportspeople of Tongan descent]] [[Category:Brisbane Broncos players]] [[Category:Fifita family|David]] [[Category:Gold Coast Titans players]] [[Category:Indigenous All Stars players]] [[Category:Ipswich Jets players]] [[Category:Prime Minister's XIII players]] [[Category:Queensland Rugby League State of Origin players]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Brisbane]] [[Category:Rugby league second-rows]] [[Category:Souths Logan Magpies players]] [[Category:Tonga national rugby league team players]] [[Category:Tongan rugby league players]] [[Category:South Sydney Rabbitohs players]]
